titleStmt.title: Letter from Nora O'Sullivan to Ellen Daly, 4 April 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Nora O'Sullivan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
P41/4/1/1
Listowel
4. 4. 23 Dear Mrs Daly How can I in this
awful hour of our great
sorrow write to you, But my heart is
yearning for one word
of consolation from you,
your letter gave me
such consolation, when
you wrote last time,
2)
I often think to myself
and ask, I wonder how
is Mrs Daly feeling, I try
to bear up like you, for
I know you are brave
you feel so closely
connected to me now,
Isn't our two brave boys
sleepin side by side
they shared each other's
joy's or sorrow's, everybody
in Donegal was so kind
and good to them
3)
They all spoke of how
bravely they went to their
death, But I never can
think they are dead atall,
Dear Mrs Daly I am
sending you a copy of
Timmy's letters, I would
like to get one from you,
I would also like to get
a photo as I will frame
them together, I will
send you a Memorial
card when I will get them
4)
Oh Dear, wasn't it awful
when I think of it to
take their young life
when we thought they
were safe. But God knows
best, I hope you will
pray for me to be consoled
and I will pray for you
and your dear boy. The
good God is the best
friend now. I remain, Dear Mrs. Daly Yours Sincerely Mrs. Nora O'Sullivan
William Street
Listowel
